entries.issue: SILENT CORRUPTION: Key implies IntelLabs Distiller knowledge-distillation documentation (https://intellabs.github.io/distiller/knowledge_distillation.html, referenced in bdsk-url-1), but the entry body is the OSF preprint 'Neural Network Compression and Knowledge Distillation: Tutorial and Survey' by Ghojogh & Ghodsi (doi 10.31219/osf.io/4n2cb). Citations of [@intellabs2023knowledge] render as '(Ghojogh and Ghodsi, 2024)' which misleads readers expecting the Intel Labs tool. Also: year in key (2023) does not match content date (2024-10-15).

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation_note: Run grep -rn 'intellabs2023knowledge' book/quarto/contents/vol1 to find call sites; rewrite to ghojogh2024distillation OR replace the entry body with a real IntelLabs Distiller webpage entry (@misc) and keep the original key.
suggested_fix.alternate_fix: If the citation truly intends the Intel Labs Distiller docs, replace the body with: title={Knowledge Distillation - Neural Network Distiller}, author={{Intel Labs}}, year={2023}, url={https://intellabs.github.io/distiller/knowledge_distillation.html}, howpublished={Software documentation}.

entries.issue: Schema: has BOTH `journal` and `booktitle` fields (entry type is @inproceedings). The journal field 'Proceedings of the IEEE Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)' duplicates booktitle. Per BibTeX convention, @inproceedings should not carry `journal`. Also missing top-level `year` field (only `date = {2018-06}` present).

Key is misleading: 'jenkins' appears to refer to the third author (W.K. Jenkins) of an ICASSP'99 paper on adaptive filtering by Soni/Gallivan/Jenkins. The key is almost certainly a copy-paste artifact (real intent was probably the Jenkins CI/CD tool, given other entries like @kserve, @kubeflow, @kubernetes nearby). The cited paper is real and verified, but the key tells readers nothing useful and any citation [@jenkins] in prose context discussing CI/CD would be silent corruption. Also: schema has both `journal = {ICASSP}` and `booktitle` — drop `journal`.

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation_note: Run grep -rn '@jenkins' book/quarto/contents/vol1 to verify call sites. If [@jenkins] is being used to cite the Jenkins automation server, the entry needs to be REPLACED with a Jenkins.io @misc entry, not just renamed.

entries.issue: Title field is just 'Caffe' with the actual descriptive title relegated to a `subtitle` field ('Convolutional Architecture for Fast Feature Embedding'). Most BibTeX styles render only `title`, so the bibliography prints as just 'Caffe'. Should merge into title = {Caffe: Convolutional Architecture for Fast Feature Embedding}. Also missing top-level `year` field.

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation_note: Set title = {Caffe: {Convolutional} Architecture for Fast Feature Embedding}; add year = {2014}; drop subtitle field.

entries.issue: Missing top-level `year` field (has only `date = {2018-07-14}`). Per §4 schema, year is required. The cited work is a real arXiv preprint; the actual MLSys 2019 publication exists as well — entry currently cites the preprint only, which is acceptable but should still carry year={2018}.

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation_note: Add year = {2018}. Optionally upgrade the entry to the MLSys 2019 conference proceedings record if the chapter cites the formal version.

entries.issue: Schema: entry type is @inproceedings but `booktitle = {arXiv preprint arXiv:1807.11205}` — arXiv preprints should be @misc or @article with archiveprefix=arXiv, not @inproceedings. Title in entry omits ': Training ImageNet in Four Minutes' subtitle that appears in the actual paper.

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation_note: Change @inproceedings to @misc; replace booktitle with note={arXiv:1807.11205} or use eprint={1807.11205} archiveprefix={arXiv}; restore full title 'Highly Scalable Deep Learning Training System with Mixed-Precision: Training ImageNet in Four Minutes'.

entries.issue: Two distinct works merged. The arXiv URL/eprint (1901.11211) is Weiwen Jiang et al.'s 2019 DAC paper 'Accuracy vs. Efficiency: ...'. But DOI 10.1002/9783527829026.ch13 with isbn 9783527348091 and pages 351-375 corresponds to a Wiley book chapter (a different artifact, possibly a re-publication or an unrelated chapter). Also has both `journal = {arXiv preprint}` and a Wiley `publisher`. Entry should pick one canonical form (DAC 2019 conference paper is the strongest).

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation_note: Replace with DAC 2019 entry: @inproceedings{jiang2019accuracy, booktitle = {Proceedings of the 56th Annual Design Automation Conference (DAC)}, publisher = {ACM}, year = {2019}, doi = {10.1145/3316781.3317757}, ...}. Drop the Wiley DOI and isbn (different work).

entries.issue: Schema: has BOTH `journal = {Journal of Machine Learning Research}` and `booktitle = {Proceedings of the 15th Conference of the European Chapter of the Association for Computational Linguistics: Volume 2, Short Papers}`. Entry is @inproceedings (correct — paper is at EACL 2017, NOT JMLR). Drop the journal field. Also missing top-level `year = {2017}` (only date present).

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation_note: Drop `journal = {Journal of Machine Learning Research}`; add `year = {2017}`. Volume = 18 with this booktitle is meaningless — drop volume too.

entries.issue: Schema: has BOTH `journal = {Communications of the ACM}` and `booktitle = {2021 ACM/IEEE 48th Annual International Symposium on Computer Architecture (ISCA)}`. Entry type is @inproceedings; the cited paper IS the ISCA 2021 paper (DOI 10.1109/isca52012.2021.00010 confirms). Drop the journal field. Also missing top-level `year`.

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation_note: Drop `journal = {Communications of the ACM}`; add `year = {2021}`. Volume/number 64/5 are CACM artifacts and should be dropped.

entries.issue: SILENT CORRUPTION: Key 'kaggle_website' implies kaggle.com (the data-science platform), but the entry body is the RKaggle R package on CRAN by Benjamin Smith (doi 10.32614/cran.package.rkaggle, title 'CRAN: Contributed Packages'). Citations of [@kaggle_website] render as '(Smith)' pointing to an unrelated R package. Almost certainly auto-stamped from CrossRef onto the wrong row.

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation_note: REPLACE entry body with the actual Kaggle homepage citation: title = {Kaggle}, author = {{Kaggle}}, year = {2024}, url = {https://www.kaggle.com/}, howpublished = {Online platform}, note = {Accessed: 2024-01-15}. Keep key as kaggle_website.

entries.issue: Author field has inconsistent name format: 'Wonyoung Kim and Gupta, Meeta S. and Wei, Gu-Yeon and Brooks, David'. First author is given as 'First Last' while the rest are 'Last, First'. BibTeX may parse 'Wonyoung Kim' as a single token in some renderers. Per §4a, should be 'Kim, Wonyoung'.

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation_note: Change first author to `Kim, Wonyoung`; full author list becomes `Kim, Wonyoung and Gupta, Meeta S. and Wei, Gu-Yeon and Brooks, David`.

entries.issue: Key year = 2017 but entry `year = {2016}`. The book was first published 2017 (O'Reilly). Per §5 the year mismatch is within 1 year (2016 vs 2017) so the key/year check tolerates it, but the field should reflect actual publication: 2017.

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation_note: Update year = {2017} to match key and actual publication date.

entries.issue: Author field is just 'Kung' — no first name or initial. Per §4a, full first name should be used when canonically attested. This is H. T. Kung (Hsiang-Tsung Kung), as confirmed by the IEEE Computer 1982 record.

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation_note: Set author = {Kung, H. T.} (or {Kung, Hsiang-Tsung} for full form). Note this entry has the same first author as @kung1979systolic; consider whether one of these duplicates the other in citations.

entries.issue: SILENT CORRUPTION (key drift): Key prefix 'Lauterbach' but entry author is 'Costa, Tiago and Shi, Chen and Tien, Kevin and Shepard, Kenneth L.' — Lauterbach is not in the author list. Verified: the cited DOI (10.1109/cicc.2019.8780236) IS the Costa et al. CICC 2019 paper on CMOS 2D beamformer for neuromodulation. Any [@Lauterbach2019] citation in prose renders as '(Costa et al., 2019)' which silently mismatches the prose context if author was named.

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation_note: Run grep -rn 'Lauterbach2019' book/quarto/contents/vol1 — likely zero or a few hits. If chapter prose names 'Lauterbach', the entry needs to be REPLACED (not renamed) with the actual Lauterbach 2019 paper the prose intends. Otherwise rename key to costa2019cmos.

entries.issue: Missing top-level `year` field. Has `date = {1989-12}` but per §4 schema and the project's bibtex-tidy build, `year` is the canonical field.

entries.note: Key year 1990 vs entry year 1989 is the standard NIPS preprint/proceedings gap (NIPS'89 conference, 1990 proceedings publication); difference is 1 year so per §5 the key-content check tolerates this silently.

entries.issue: Key year is 2022 but content date is 2021-07-14 (arXiv preprint v2). The paper was published at ACL 2022 (Findings). The arXiv date is 2021 but conference is 2022 — within 1-year preprint gap, so the key/year check tolerates it. Missing top-level `year` field, which IS required per schema. Status uncertain because the entry could plausibly be upgraded to the ACL 2022 conference record.

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation_note: Add year = {2022}. Consider upgrading to the ACL 2022 publication: @inproceedings{...booktitle = {Proceedings of the 60th Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics}, year = {2022}, doi = {10.18653/v1/2022.acl-long.577}, ...}.

entries.issue: `author = {Lepikhin, Dmitry and others}` — full author list should be expanded per §4a (no 'and others' for primary published works when full byline is known). Authoritative byline: Dmitry Lepikhin, HyoukJoong Lee, Yuanzhong Xu, Dehao Chen, Orhan Firat, Yanping Huang, Maxim Krikun, Noam Shazeer, Zhifeng Chen. (Year mismatch key=2020 vs field=2021 is acceptable — preprint vs ICLR, within 1-year tolerance.)

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation_note: Expand author = {Lepikhin, Dmitry and Lee, HyoukJoong and Xu, Yuanzhong and Chen, Dehao and Firat, Orhan and Huang, Yanping and Krikun, Maxim and Shazeer, Noam and Chen, Zhifeng}.

entries.issue: Could not locate this paper in 3 searches. Title 'A Survey on Memory Management Strategies for Machine Learning Systems' by 'Li, Z. and Wang, X. and Zhang, Y.' in ACM Computing Surveys vol 54 no 6 pages 1-30 — no DOI/URL provided in entry. Author names are suspiciously generic (single-letter initials, three of the most common Chinese surnames). ACM CSUR vol 54 issue 6 (2022) does NOT appear to contain such a survey per dblp/ACM DL listings. Likely fictional / fabricated entry, but cannot fully confirm without exhaustive search.

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation_note: Manually verify on ACM Digital Library — search ACM CSUR vol 54 issue 6 contents. If not found, this entry is fabricated and any [@li2021survey] citation needs to be replaced with a real memory-management ML systems survey (e.g., https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3494523 — Padon & Shavit, A Survey of ML for Computer Architecture and Systems, ACM CSUR 2022).

entries.note: Key 'lime_github' implies a GitHub artifact but body is the canonical KDD'16 LIME paper (Ribeiro et al.). The note field bridges the two by linking to the GitHub repo. Acceptable as a project-name key per §5 (alexnet2012, jax2018github pattern); not flagged.

top_3_broken.summary: Key implies IntelLabs Distiller knowledge-distillation docs; body is unrelated Ghojogh & Ghodsi OSF preprint. Citations render as wrong attribution.
Key implies kaggle.com; body is the unrelated RKaggle R package by Benjamin Smith. CrossRef auto-stamp on wrong row.

Key prefix 'Lauterbach' but author list is Costa/Shi/Tien/Shepard. Cited DOI is Costa et al. CICC'19 — Lauterbach not in byline. Either rename to costa2019cmos or replace body with the intended Lauterbach paper.
